_description: Meta-fixture for self-test/run.sh. Contains one case that MUST pass and one that MUST fail. Used by the runner-meta case in golden-cases.example.json to regression-test the runner itself.

cases._description: Deliberately trivial all-green script case. Exercises expected_exit, expected_stdout_jq, and expected_stderr_contains happy paths.
Deliberately broken — step 2 exits 1 where we expect 0. Exercises the failure-report path so runner-meta can assert nonzero exit + clear error message.
expected_stdout_contains happy path: stdout contains the needle, exit 0 — the case PASSES. Paired with assert-stdout-contains-red to prove the assertion actually runs (#250).

expected_stdout_contains RED proof: stdout is 'hello' but the step expects 'goodbye'. Exit code matches (0), so before #250 the runner IGNORED the field and the case PASSED — false confidence. After #250 the runner asserts on stdout and this case MUST FAIL (exit 1). runner-meta asserts the nonzero exit + the 'stdout did not contain' message.

steps.name: stdout missing needle should fail the case

cases._description: Unknown-key guard proof: the step carries 'expected_stduot_contains' (deliberate typo of expected_stdout_contains). Before #250 this typo was silently ignored — the step passed on exit code alone. After #250 the runner MUST FAIL the case (exit 1) with a message naming the unknown key, so typos/unsupported fields are loud not silent. runner-meta asserts the nonzero exit + the 'unknown key' message.

steps.name: step with a typo'd assertion key
